feed.title: Google Cloud: Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.33.600-gke.40:

  • Fixed an issue where if updates or upgrades to advanced admin clusters failed and the external bootstrap cluster was deleted, you could lose critical data.
entry.summary: An issue has been fixed where critical data could be lost if updates or upgrades to advanced admin clusters failed and the external bootstrap cluster was deleted. This resolves a potential data loss scenario for cluster operations.
entry.title: Fixed: Fixed critical data loss issue

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.32.1000-gke.57:

  • Fixed an issue where the node-problem-detector was incorrectly deployed onto non-Advanced (V1) VMware clusters, causing the containerd runtime to continuously restart on affected nodes, leading to ETCD/CRI failures and unsuccessful cluster upgrades.
  • Fixed an issue where setting the deprecated stackdriver.enableVPC field to true in a cluster configuration file would block upgrades to an Advanced Cluster. The stackdriver.enableVPC field has been deprecated and its setting is now ignored during the upgrade validation process.
  • Fixes an issue where Advanced Clusters incorrectly deployed the node problem detector onto non-Advanced clusters, which caused containerd to continuously restart and led to cluster upgrade failures.
  • Fixed an issue where the system certificate pool was ignored when a custom CA certificate was configured for a registry mirror.
  • Fixed an issue where retrying the gkectl upgrade admin command after a previous failure could fail with "AlreadyExists" errors in the bootstrap cluster.
  • Fixed an issue where cluster creation or upgrade failed if the proxy or noProxy configuration fields contained extraneous whitespaces. These spaces interfered with internal command-line argument parsing, causing the control plane load balancer initialization to fail.
  • Fixed an issue where if updates or upgrades to advanced admin clusters failed and the external bootstrap cluster was deleted, you could lose critical data.
entry.summary: This release addresses several critical issues impacting cluster stability, upgrades, and configuration. Fixes include resolution of node problem detector deployment errors, issues with deprecated fields blocking upgrades, incorrect certificate handling, and failures during cluster creation and upgrade processes.
entry.title: Fixed: Resolved cluster issues

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.32.1000-gke.57 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade clusters. Google Distributed Cloud 1.32.1000-gke.57 runs on Kubernetes v1.32.13-gke.1000.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the Google Distributed Cloud-ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: A new version of Google Distributed Cloud for VMware (1.32.1000-gke.57) is now available. Please check storage partner qualifications and note that the update may take a few days to be fully available through Google Cloud tools.
entry.title: Announcement: New software release available

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.33.600-gke.40 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade clusters. Google Distributed Cloud 1.33.600-gke.40 runs on Kubernetes 1.33.5-gke.2200.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the Google Distributed Cloud-ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: The latest version of Google Distributed Cloud for VMware, 1.33.600-gke.40, is now ready for download. This release runs on Kubernetes 1.33.5-gke.2200. Please verify storage vendor compatibility and be aware that full API client support may take 7-14 days to become available.
entry.title: Announcement: New VMware Release Available

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.33.600-gke.39 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade clusters. Google Distributed Cloud 1.33.600-gke.39 runs on Kubernetes v1.33.5-gke.2200.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the Google Distributed Cloud-ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.33.600-gke.39 is now available for download. This release runs on Kubernetes v1.33.5-gke.2200. Check the storage partners document if you use third-party storage.
entry.title: Announcement: New version of Distributed Cloud for VMware available

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.33.600-gke.39:

  • Fixed an issue where the node-problem-detector was incorrectly deployed onto non-Advanced (V1) VMware clusters, causing the containerd runtime to continuously restart on affected nodes, leading to ETCD/CRI failures and unsuccessful cluster upgrades.
  • Fixed an issue where setting the deprecated stackdriver.enableVPC field to true in a cluster configuration file would block upgrades to an Advanced Cluster. The stackdriver.enableVPC field has been deprecated and its setting is now ignored during the upgrade validation process.
  • Fixes an issue where Advanced Clusters incorrectly deployed the node problem detector onto non-Advanced clusters, which caused containerd to continuously restart and led to cluster upgrade failures.
  • Fixed an issue where retrying the gkectl upgrade admin command after a previous failure could fail with "AlreadyExists" errors in the bootstrap cluster.
  • Fixed an issue where cluster creation or upgrade failed if the proxy or noProxy configuration fields contained extraneous whitespaces. These spaces interfered with internal command-line argument parsing, causing the control plane load balancer initialization to fail.
  • Fixed an issue where the system certificate pool was ignored when a custom CA certificate was configured for a registry mirror.
entry.summary: This release addresses several critical issues, including incorrect deployment of the node-problem-detector on certain cluster types, problems with upgrade blockers related to deprecated fields, and failures during cluster creation or upgrade due to proxy configuration errors. These fixes ensure smoother cluster operations and upgrades.
entry.title: Fixed: Resolved cluster upgrade and runtime issues

entry.content:
Important: Mandatory flag for admin cluster upgrades

If an update or upgrade to advanced admin clusters fails in versions 1.32 and newer, don't delete the external bootstrap cluster from the workstation. The bootstrap cluster contains required information about states that you need to resume the update or upgrade. If an update or upgrade to admin clusters fails, and you re-run gkectl upgrade admin, you must add the flag --reuse-bootstrap-cluster or you can lose critical data.
entry.summary: Updates to admin cluster upgrades require a new flag to prevent data loss if an upgrade fails. If an upgrade fails and is re-run, use the --reuse-bootstrap-cluster flag.
entry.title: Change: Admin cluster upgrade changes

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.34.200-gke.68:

  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
  • Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware V2 (Advanced Clusters) versions 1.31 and earlier were missing a configuration step in the node startup script that defined the Docker default bridge IP range. As a result, Docker defaulted to using the 172.17.0.0/16 (and in some cases 172.16.0.0/16) address range.
  • Fixed an issue where an admin cluster upgrade appeared to be stuck indefinitely and users would see the VSphereMachine remaining in the Creating phase without actionable error messages.
entry.summary: This release addresses security vulnerabilities and resolves issues related to node startup configuration and cluster upgrades. It ensures a more stable and secure environment for your deployments.
entry.title: Fixed: Security and Stability Improvements

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.34.200-gke.68 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade clusters.

Important: There is a mandatory flag for admin cluster upgrades; see the updated entry for March 20, 2026.

Google Distributed Cloud 1.34.200-gke.68 runs on Kubernetes v1.34.3-gke.400.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the Google Distributed Cloud-ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: The latest software-only version of Google Distributed Cloud for VMware, 1.34.200-gke.68, is now ready for download. This release runs on Kubernetes v1.34.3-gke.400. Please review important upgrade notes, including a mandatory flag for admin cluster upgrades, before proceeding.
entry.title: Announcement: New software version available

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.32.900-gke.60:

  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
entry.summary: This release addresses security vulnerabilities. The fixes are detailed in the vulnerability fixes documentation.
entry.title: Fixed: Security fixes applied

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.32.900-gke.60 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade clusters.

Important: There is a mandatory flag for admin cluster upgrades; see the updated entry for March 20, 2026.

Google Distributed Cloud 1.32.900-gke.60 runs on Kubernetes v1.32.11-gke.200.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the Google Distributed Cloud-ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: The latest version of Google Distributed Cloud for VMware (1.32.900-gke.60) is now ready for download. Please review the upgrade documentation for instructions and important notes regarding admin cluster upgrades. Ensure your third-party storage is qualified for this release.
entry.title: Announcement: New software release available

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware V2 (Advanced Clusters) versions 1.31 and earlier were missing a configuration step in the node startup script that defined the Docker default bridge IP range. As a result, Docker defaulted to using the 172.17.0.0/16 (and in some cases 172.16.0.0/16) address range.

If this default range overlaps with customer network infrastructure, connectivity failures during cluster creation or operation can occur.

This issue has been resolved. The Docker default bridge IP for cluster nodes in advanced clusters is now explicitly set to 169.254.123.1/24.
entry.summary: An issue with node startup scripts in VMware Advanced Clusters has been resolved. The Docker default bridge IP range was not explicitly defined, potentially causing network conflicts. This has been corrected by setting the Docker default bridge IP to 169.254.123.1/24 for cluster nodes.
entry.title: Change: Node Startup Script Update for VMware Advanced Clusters

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.33.500-gke.63:

  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
entry.summary: Version 1.33.500-gke.63 addresses several security vulnerabilities. This update ensures improved security for your environment by patching known issues.
entry.title: Fixed: Security fixes applied

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware V2 (Advanced Clusters) versions 1.31 and earlier were missing a configuration step in the node startup script that defined the Docker default bridge IP range. As a result, Docker defaulted to using the 172.17.0.0/16 (and in some cases 172.16.0.0/16) address range.

If this default range overlaps with customer network infrastructure, connectivity failures during cluster creation or operation can occur.

This issue has been resolved. The Docker default bridge IP for cluster nodes in advanced clusters is now explicitly set to 169.254.123.1/24.
entry.summary: An issue where Docker's default IP range could conflict with customer networks in Google Distributed Cloud for VMware has been fixed. The Docker default bridge IP for advanced cluster nodes is now explicitly set to 169.254.123.1/24, preventing potential connectivity failures during cluster operations.
entry.title: Announcement: Docker IP Range Issue Resolved

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.33.500-gke.63 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade clusters.

Important: There is a mandatory flag for admin cluster upgrades; see the updated entry for March 20, 2026.

Google Distributed Cloud 1.33.500-gke.63 runs on Kubernetes v1.33.5-gke.2200.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the Google Distributed Cloud-ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: Google Distributed Cloud for VMware 1.33.500-gke.63 is now available for download. This release runs on Kubernetes v1.33.5-gke.2200 and includes important updates. Please review the upgrade documentation and check for storage partner compatibility before upgrading.
entry.title: Announcement: New version of Distributed Cloud for VMware released

entry.content: Upgrading to advanced clusters overwrites any existing cert-manager. For more information, see Upgrade to cert-manager bundled with advanced clusters.
entry.summary: Upgrading to advanced clusters will overwrite your existing cert-manager installation. Please review the documentation for instructions on how to manage cert-manager during this process.
entry.title: Breaking: cert-manager overwrites on advanced cluster upgrade

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.34.100-gke.93 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade a cluster. Google Distributed Cloud 1.34.100-gke.93 runs on Kubernetes v1.34.1-gke.4700.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the Google Distributed Cloud-ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: Google Distributed Cloud for VMware 1.34.100-gke.93 is now available. Check storage partner qualifications and note the rollout period for API clients.
entry.title: Announcement: New VMware Version Available

entry.content:
The following feature was added in 1.34.100-gke.93:

You can deploy vsphere-csi-controller in the advanced cluster on the user cluster control plane nodes.
entry.summary: You can now deploy the vsphere-csi-controller directly on the control plane nodes of your user cluster. This feature is available in version 1.34.100-gke.93 and simplifies the deployment of CSI drivers for vSphere environments.
entry.title: Feature: Deploy vsphere-csi-controller on user cluster control plane nodes

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.32.800-gke.126 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade clusters. Google Distributed Cloud 1.32.800-gke.126 runs on Kubernetes v1.32.11-gke.200.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the Google Distributed Cloud-ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: A new version of Google Distributed Cloud for VMware (1.32.800-gke.126) is now available for download. Ensure your third-party storage is qualified for this release. The update will be available via Google Cloud console, gcloud CLI, and Terraform within 7-14 days.
entry.title: Announcement: New version of VMware software available

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.33.400-gke.113 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade clusters. Google Distributed Cloud 1.33.400-gke.113 runs on Kubernetes v1.33.5-gke.1900.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the Google Distributed Cloud-ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: Google Distributed Cloud for VMware version 1.33.400-gke.113 is now available for download. This release runs on Kubernetes v1.33.5-gke.1900. Please check the storage partners document if you use third-party storage, and note that the version may take 7-14 days to be available via Google Cloud console and gcloud CLI.
entry.title: Announcement: New Software Version Available

entry.content:
Multiple security vulnerabilities have been identified in the OpenSSL library. The most significant finding is CVE-2025-15467, a critical vulnerability that might allow for remote code execution (RCE) or denial of service (DoS) attacks via network-based vectors.

For more details, see the GCP-2026-006 security bulletin.
entry.summary: Critical security vulnerabilities have been found in the OpenSSL library, including CVE-2025-15467, which could lead to remote code execution or denial of service attacks. Users are advised to review the security bulletin for detailed information and mitigation steps.
entry.title: Security: OpenSSL Security Vulnerabilities Identified

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.31.1200-gke.60 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade a cluster. Google Distributed Cloud 1.31.1200-gke.60 runs on Kubernetes v1.31.13-gke.1100.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the GDC Ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: The latest software-only version of Google Distributed Cloud for VMware, 1.31.1200-gke.60, is now available for download. This release runs on Kubernetes v1.31.13-gke.1100 and includes important updates. Ensure your third-party storage vendor is qualified for this release before upgrading. Please note that new versions may take 7-14 days to become available through GKE On-Prem API clients.
entry.title: Announcement: New Software Version Released

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.34.0-gke.566:

  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
entry.summary: This release resolves security vulnerabilities. For a detailed list of fixed issues, please refer to the documentation on vulnerability fixes.
entry.title: Fixed: Vulnerabilities addressed

entry.content:
• Upgraded containerd from 1.7 to 2.0.
  • Registry mirror configuration information has been migrated to the hosts.toml containerd config file.
  • Upgrade preflight checks now validate PodDisruptionBudgets.
entry.summary: Containerd has been upgraded to version 2.0. This includes migrating registry mirror configurations to hosts.toml and enhancing preflight checks to validate PodDisruptionBudgets.
entry.title: Change: Containerd 2.0 Upgrade Complete

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.34.0-gke.566 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade a cluster. Google Distributed Cloud 1.34.0-gke.566 runs on Kubernetes v1.34.1-gke.2900.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the GDC Ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: The latest version of Google Distributed Cloud for VMware, 1.34.0-gke.566, is now available for download and upgrade. This release runs on Kubernetes v1.34.1-gke.2900. Please ensure your third-party storage is compatible before upgrading.
entry.title: Announcement: New release of VMware software available

entry.content: Changed the upgrade logic for non-advanced clusters. When you upgrade a non-advanced cluster from version 1.33 to 1.34, the cluster is automatically converted to an advanced cluster. If you use custom code that depends on the non-advanced cluster configuration, this can be a breaking change. For more information, see Key differences after moving to advanced clusters.
entry.summary: Upgrading non-advanced clusters from 1.33 to 1.34 will now automatically convert them to advanced clusters. This change might affect custom code relying on the previous configuration. Review the documentation for details on advanced cluster differences.
entry.title: Breaking: Upgrade logic change for non-advanced clusters

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.33.300-gke.60:

  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
entry.summary: This release addresses security vulnerabilities.
entry.title: Fixed: Security vulnerabilities fixed

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.33.300-gke.60 is available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade a cluster. Distributed Cloud 1.33.300-gke.60 runs on Kubernetes v1.33.5-gke.900.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the GDC Ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: The latest version of Google Distributed Cloud for VMware (1.33.300-gke.60) is now available for download and upgrade. This release runs on Kubernetes v1.33.5-gke.900. Please ensure your third-party storage vendor is qualified for this release before upgrading. Note that GKE On-Prem API clients will have access to this version in 7-14 days.
entry.title: Announcement: Google Distributed Cloud for VMware 1.33.300-gke.60 Release

entry.content:

Please check the GDC Ready storage partners document if you use a third-party storage vendor. Note that the new version may take 7-14 days to be available for GKE On-Prem API clients.
entry.title: Announcement: New release of Google Distributed Cloud for VMware

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.32.700-gke.64:

  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
entry.summary: This release includes fixes for reported vulnerabilities. For more details, please refer to the vulnerability fixes documentation.
entry.title: Fixed: Vulnerability fixes in 1.32.700-gke.64

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.33.200-gke.70:

  • Fixed an issue that gkectl update blocks updating a non-HA non-advanced cluster to HA advanced cluster
  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
entry.summary: Resolved an issue where `gkectl update` prevented non-HA to HA advanced cluster updates. Additionally, addressed vulnerabilities mentioned in the security fixes documentation.
entry.title: Fixed: Fixed cluster update issue

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.31.1100-gke.40 is available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade a cluster. Distributed Cloud 1.31.1100-gke.40 runs on Kubernetes v1.31.12-gke.600.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the GDC Ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: The new version of Google Distributed Cloud for VMware (1.31.1100-gke.40) is now available for download and upgrade. This release runs on Kubernetes v1.31.12-gke.600. Please check the GDC Ready storage partners document if you are using a third-party storage vendor. Note that GKE On-Prem API clients will have access to this version in 7-14 days.

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.31.1100-gke.40:

  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
entry.summary: The latest release includes fixes for security vulnerabilities.
entry.title: Fixed: Security Updates

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.31.1000-gke.44:

  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
entry.summary: A new release is available with fixes for reported vulnerabilities.
entry.title: Fixed: Vulnerabilities Fixed

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.33.100-gke.89 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade a cluster. Distributed Cloud 1.33.100-gke.89 runs on Kubernetes v1.33.4-gke.900.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the GDC Ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.33.100-gke.89:

  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
entry.summary: This release addresses security vulnerabilities.
entry.title: Fixed: Security vulnerabilities fixed

The following issues were fixed in 1.32.500-gke.48:

  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
entry.summary: Resolved security vulnerabilities in version 1.32.500-gke.48.
entry.title: Fixed: Vulnerabilities Fixed

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.31.900-gke.38 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade a cluster. Distributed Cloud 1.31.900-gke.38 runs on Kubernetes v1.31.12-gke.200.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the GDC Ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: The latest version of Google Distributed Cloud for VMware (1.31.900-gke.38) is now available for download. This release runs on Kubernetes v1.31.12-gke.200. Please check the GDC Ready storage partners document if you are using a third-party storage vendor. Note that it may take 7-14 days for the new version to be available via Google Cloud console, gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.title: Announcement: Google Distributed Cloud for VMware 1.31.900-gke.38 Released

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.31.900-gke.38:

  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
entry.summary: This release addresses security vulnerabilities.
entry.title: Fixed: Vulnerabilities Fixed

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.33.0-gke.799 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade a cluster. Distributed Cloud 1.33.0-gke.799 runs on Kubernetes v1.33.2-gke.700.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the GDC Ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.33.0-gke.799 is now available. This release runs on Kubernetes v1.33.2-gke.700. Check partner storage qualifications if using third-party vendors. Updates via GKE On-Prem API clients will be available in 7-14 days.
entry.title: Announcement: Google Distributed Cloud for VMware 1.33.0-gke.799 Released

entry.content:
• GA: Changed the cluster creation process so that all new clusters are advanced clusters. Additionally, all cluster upgrades to 1.33 are automatically converted to advanced clusters.
  • Upgraded the etcd component to version 3.4.33.
entry.summary: All new clusters are advanced clusters. Upgrades to 1.33 are automatically converted to advanced clusters. The etcd component has been upgraded to version 3.4.33.
entry.title: Changed: New advanced clusters

entry.content:
• GA: Enabled the vsphere-metrics-exporter component for advanced clusters. This exporter provides greater visibility into the VMware vSphere environment by collecting key performance and health metrics.
  • GA: Added support for VM-Host affinity groups in advanced clusters. This feature allows for the creation of rules that constrain cluster nodes to run on specific, predefined groups of hosts.
  • GA: Added support for automatic node resizing in advanced clusters. This feature optimizes resource use by automatically adjusting the CPU and memory allocated to control plane nodes in response to workload demands.
  • Public Preview: Added support for Virtual Machine (VM) tracking using vSphere tags in advanced clusters. This feature simplifies resource management by automatically applying identifying tags to cluster VMs.
  • GA: Introduced an Envoy proxy sidecar to the GKE Identity Service for clusters that use Controlplane V2. This change enhances the security, reliability, and performance of the authentication service.
entry.summary: Advanced clusters now support VM-Host affinity, automatic node resizing, and the vsphere-metrics-exporter for better VMware visibility. VM tracking with vSphere tags is in public preview. Envoy proxy enhances GKE Identity Service security.
entry.title: Feature: New features for advanced clusters

entry.content:
The following issues were fixed in 1.33.0-gke.799:

  • Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes.
entry.summary: Fixed vulnerabilities listed in Vulnerability fixes documentation.
entry.title: Fixed: Fixed vulnerabilities

entry.content:
Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.32.400-gke.68 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade a cluster. Google Distributed Cloud 1.32.400-gke.68 runs on Kubernetes v1.32.7-gke.200.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the GDC Ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.

Users with third-party storage should verify compatibility with GDC Ready partners. Google Distributed Cloud (software only) for VMware 1.30.1200-gke.63 is now available for download. To upgrade, see Upgrade a cluster. Google Distributed Cloud 1.30.1200-gke.63 runs on Kubernetes v1.30.12-gke.1200. This is the final patch for the 1.30 minor release.

If you are using a third-party storage vendor, check the GDC Ready storage partners document to make sure the storage vendor has already passed the qualification for this release.

After a release, it takes approximately 7 to 14 days for the version to become available for use with GKE On-Prem API clients: the Google Cloud console, the gcloud CLI, and Terraform.
entry.summary: Google Distributed Cloud for VMware 1.30.1200-gke.63 has been released and is available for download. This version runs on Kubernetes v1.30.12-gke.1200 and is the final patch for the 1.30 minor release. Please check storage partner qualifications if applicable. The new version will be available via GKE On-Prem API clients within 7-14 days.
entry.title: Announcement: New Release Available

entry.title: Announcement: New Google Distributed Cloud for VMware Release

entry.content: For clusters configured with advanced clusters, introduced an Envoy sidecar into the GKE Identity Service to increase security, reliability, and performance.
entry.summary: The GKE Identity Service for advanced clusters now includes an Envoy sidecar to enhance security, reliability, and performance.
entry.title: Changed: GKE Identity Service Security Update
